title: Life on Mars? Organic Material Found
description: Mark Salvatore, an NAU planetary scientist who collaborates with NASA's Curiosity rover team, unpacks the agency's finding of organic molecules and methane in Martian rocks. He describes how the Sample Analysis at Mars (SAM) instrument heats drilled material and reads the chemical signatures that reveal organics, why those molecules matter as building blocks of life without proving biology, and how their preservation in sedimentary rocks supports ancient habitable conditions. Salvatore also notes why present-day life, if it exists, would more likely be sheltered deep below the radiation-blasted surface.
impact: Radiant advances planetary remote sensing by connecting NAU researchers to active Mars exploration and interpretation. Work like Salvatore's helps translate rover data into a deeper understanding of planetary surfaces, past habitability, and the search for meaningful chemical signatures beyond Earth.
